table_introductions.table_introduction: This table maintains master data for retail and parlor clients, capturing their identification details, location, associated sales representatives, distribution sources, and operational assignments. Key fields include client and distributor IDs, addresses, employee and region details, visit and order dates, and verification status. It is used to track client coverage, manage field sales assignments, analyze visit frequency, and support territory or performance reporting.

This table records detailed sales transaction data, capturing information about orders, clients, products, and associated employees. It includes amounts, discounts, taxes, product details, and hierarchical sales designations for tracking transaction flow from distributors to retailers. Analysts use it to monitor sales performance, evaluate discount impacts, and generate reports segmented by region, product category, or sales staff.

This table records daily activity and attendance details for field employees, capturing information about their work routes, visit timings, locations, travel distances, and performance metrics. Key fields include employee identifiers, attendance status, territory details (such as city, zone, and beats), visit start and end times, total working and field hours, and associated productivity indicators. It is useful for tracking workforce movement, analyzing field efficiency, and monitoring attendance and regional performance trends.

This table captures detailed information on product orders at the SKU level, linking each transaction to the distributor, client, sales employee, and route (beat) details. It includes product attributes, pricing, quantities, order amounts, dates, and associated distribution hierarchies. Analysts use this table to monitor sales performance, evaluate distributor and salesperson productivity, and analyze product-level sales trends across territories and time periods.

This table contains consolidated stock and sales performance data for clients across different zones and product categories. Each record captures product details, client information, and quantitative metrics such as opening and closing stock, received and returned quantities, sales values, and stock adjustments. It is used to monitor inventory movements, assess sales trends, and evaluate channel-level performance for retail and parlor clients.
